insight: Rich symptom timeline and patient narrative intake
problem_statement: Short, single-symptom forms and rushed histories lose duration, severity, patient concern, prior failed visits, and functional impact before clinician review.
evidence_basis: Patient-flow and clinician packages flag incomplete history and single-symptom routing; patient complaints show dismissal and unexplained symptoms; missed-signal cases tie patient voice/severity capture to delayed diagnosis patterns.
interpretation: Structured + free-text intake: timeline builder, “what changed from normal?”, “what worries you most?”, impact on daily life, prior visits/tests, patient theory, and optional photos/documents.
contradictions_or_limits: No direct contradiction found in packages; still requires prospective validation.
next_evidence_needed: Workflow test with clinicians/patients; measure closed-loop completion, clinician time, alert burden, patient trust, and missed/escalated cases.

problem_statement: Hidden, chronic, fluctuating, and rare conditions may look non-urgent at a single visit unless the system captures deterioration from the patient’s personal baseline.
evidence_basis: Cases around endometriosis, POTS/dysautonomia, ME/CFS functional decline, CKD stories, rare disease delays, and clinician time-pressure sources point to baseline change and function as diagnostic signals.
interpretation: Baseline-change module with patient-selected anchors: work/school/activity, sleep, eating/drinking, mobility, self-care, and symptom-free baseline.
contradictions_or_limits: No direct contradiction found in packages; still requires prospective validation.
next_evidence_needed: Workflow test with clinicians/patients; measure closed-loop completion, clinician time, alert burden, patient trust, and missed/escalated cases.

problem_statement: Prior cancer, family history, previous diagnoses, and caregiver observations can exist in records or family reports but fail to influence the current diagnostic frame.
evidence_basis: The Brian continuity-of-care case centered on prior cancer plus repeated back pain not being connected; patient-complaint cases include prior cancer and family-history signals ignored.
interpretation: Persistent high-risk history card that attaches to symptom categories and repeat visits; caregiver/family-history prompt when relevant.
contradictions_or_limits: No direct contradiction found in packages; still requires prospective validation.
next_evidence_needed: Workflow test with clinicians/patients; measure closed-loop completion, clinician time, alert burden, patient trust, and missed/escalated cases.

insight: Medication, access, and affordability as diagnostic clues
problem_statement: Medication interruption, inability to pay, lack of transport, or supply/device problems may cause deterioration or block diagnostic completion but are often hidden in clinical notes.
evidence_basis: Missed-signal product opportunities include medication/access as a clue; global and low-resource evidence shows cost, transport, and diagnostic access shape whether conditions are identified.
interpretation: Sensitive access-risk field with options for cost, transport, stockout, insurance, medicine interruption, device shortage, and preferred support referral.
contradictions_or_limits: Evidence supports this in some system types; may not fit integrated high-resource systems or settings without referral capacity. Potential safety/ethics concern; requires clinical governance and local calibration.
next_evidence_needed: Workflow test with clinicians/patients; measure closed-loop completion, clinician time, alert burden, patient trust, and missed/escalated cases.

insight: Longitudinal unresolved-problem tracker / diagnostic episode layer
problem_statement: Multiple visits, labs, referrals, messages, and symptoms are treated as separate transactions, so no one sees the unresolved problem as one diagnostic episode.
evidence_basis: All five packages repeatedly identify repeat visits, no follow-up ownership, and no-one-connected-the-dots as black zones; clinician and global packages explicitly recommend a longitudinal unresolved-problem tracker or diagnostic episode layer.
interpretation: Diagnostic episode object that clusters unresolved symptoms, visits, tests, referrals, patient check-ins, and open questions until a clinician closes or reframes it.
contradictions_or_limits: Potential safety/ethics concern; requires clinical governance and local calibration.
next_evidence_needed: Workflow test with clinicians/patients; measure closed-loop completion, clinician time, alert burden, patient trust, and missed/escalated cases.

evidence_basis: Jess’s Rule, Brian continuity case, ED/primary-care cases, and Research B tag counts show repeated visits ignored across settings.
interpretation: Automatic repeat-presentation banner with prior diagnosis, unresolved symptom, what changed, dangerous alternatives, and required safety-net plan.
contradictions_or_limits: No direct contradiction found in packages; still requires prospective validation.
next_evidence_needed: Workflow test with clinicians/patients; measure closed-loop completion, clinician time, alert burden, patient trust, and missed/escalated cases.

insight: Normal-test safety net and test-scope explainer
problem_statement: A normal or incomplete test can be mistaken for diagnostic closure even when symptoms persist or the test does not rule out the feared condition.
evidence_basis: Normal-test trap appears across ED, screening, primary care, chronic disease, and missed-signal case libraries; patient complaints include severe pain after normal bloods/X-ray and PE after clear CXR.
interpretation: For selected tests, show date/scope/limitations, symptom status after result, “what this does not rule out,” and safety-net check-in date.
contradictions_or_limits: Potential safety/ethics concern; requires clinical governance and local calibration.
next_evidence_needed: Workflow test with clinicians/patients; measure closed-loop completion, clinician time, alert burden, patient trust, and missed/escalated cases.

interpretation: Ownership ledger: result severity, named owner, due date, acknowledgement, action plan, patient notified, escalation path, closure reason.
contradictions_or_limits: Potential safety/ethics concern; requires clinical governance and local calibration.

problem_statement: A referral being placed is often mistaken for the problem being handled, while real-world referrals can be rejected, delayed, sent to the wrong specialty, missing information, unaffordable, or never attended.

interpretation: Referral state machine: drafted, sent, received, triaged, more-info-needed, rejected, booked, attended, report returned, next-step closed; patient-visible explanation.
contradictions_or_limits: No direct contradiction found in packages; still requires prospective validation.
next_evidence_needed: Workflow test with clinicians/patients; measure closed-loop completion, clinician time, alert burden, patient trust, and missed/escalated cases.

insight: Diagnosis-status clarity and “not yet explained” state
problem_statement: Patients can leave portals or visits believing a diagnosis was ruled out, confirmed, or ignored when the clinician actually considers it unresolved or pending.
evidence_basis: Patient complaints include “still no idea,” no initial explanation, rejected referral without meeting patient, and product implications call for diagnosis-status clarity in portals.
interpretation: Problem status labels: confirmed, suspected, ruled out for now, not yet explained, pending test/referral, watchful waiting with trigger to return; patient can ask for clarification.
contradictions_or_limits: No direct contradiction found in packages; still requires prospective validation.
next_evidence_needed: Workflow test with clinicians/patients; measure closed-loop completion, clinician time, alert burden, patient trust, and missed/escalated cases.

insight: Risk-ranked result/referral worklists to reduce alert fatigue
problem_statement: Adding reminders can worsen clinician alert fatigue unless the system prioritizes meaningful diagnostic risk, ownership, and actionability.
evidence_basis: Clinician package reports median 63 EHR alerts/day and missed-result risk; risks notes warn more alerts can worsen alert fatigue; referral/result follow-up evidence supports risk-ranked queues.
interpretation: Unified risk-ranked worklist for abnormal results, referrals, pending tests, and unresolved episodes, using criticality, elapsed time, deterioration, and patient vulnerability/access risk.
contradictions_or_limits: Potential safety/ethics concern; requires clinical governance and local calibration.
next_evidence_needed: Workflow test with clinicians/patients; measure closed-loop completion, clinician time, alert burden, patient trust, and missed/escalated cases.

insight: Symptom-still-present check-ins after normal tests or watchful waiting
problem_statement: Safety-netting relies on patients knowing when/how to re-contact care and on systems noticing that symptoms persist after reassurance.
evidence_basis: Safety-netting, normal-test trap, repeated visits, and patient complaints repeatedly show persistent symptoms after a normal test or discharge can be lost.
interpretation: Timed check-in asking whether key symptoms resolved/worsened, with red-flag routing, patient-friendly return instructions, and clinician review if unresolved.
contradictions_or_limits: No direct contradiction found in packages; still requires prospective validation.
next_evidence_needed: Workflow test with clinicians/patients; measure closed-loop completion, clinician time, alert burden, patient trust, and missed/escalated cases.
